{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: ** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 35|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City official website * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=18 Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Shinichi Town Tourism Association [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Burial mounds in Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Fukuyama]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Square burial mounds]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Sites]] [[Category:Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:大佐山白塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: ** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: ** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * 35|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}}]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama {{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama revision.text:
{{{{Infobox ancient site |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square burial mound|ja|方墳}} (or {{ill|Round burial mound|ja|円墳}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square burial mound |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: ** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 35|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: ** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * 35|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|burial mound|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The burial mound is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Burial Mounds Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: ** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) *
