{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |Size = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |Construction Period = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |Map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |Label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |Label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |Latitude Degree = 34|Latitude Minute = 33|Latitude Second = 45.00 |Longitude Degree = 133|Longitude Minute = 17|Longitude Second = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |Shape = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Imperial Mausoleum = |Historical Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historical Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label Position = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historical Site. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historical Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun |Group = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-typed (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-typed (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |ISO = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |Icon = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * 35|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == other_designation == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site information board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |omitted = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) |area = 12 meters per side |burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} |Buried Individual = |Mausoleum = |other_designation = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated other_designation: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |Cultural Property = |Notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |type = Square Kofun |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designated as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designated a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designated historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama|ja|福山市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been designation1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site information board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|granite|ja|花崗岩}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was designation1 a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation in [[1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture other_designation. * [[1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the designation1 historical site. * [[1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} that opens southward. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Ōbō Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama
