Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City official website * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=18 Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 |coordinatesree = 133|coordinatesute = 17|coordinatesond = 44.68 |omitted = JP-34 |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |area = 12 meters per side |burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City official website * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=18 Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Shinichi Town Tourism Association {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|大佐山白塚古墳|version=104540395|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun]][[sn:Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ōsayama Shiratsuka Kofun]] [[ja:大佐山白塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11432371}}

|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Fukuyama, Hiroshima|Fukuyama]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]], in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City official website * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=18 Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - The chamber is finely constructed with cut [[:en:Granite|granite]], and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early [[:en:7th century|7th century]] (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * [[:en:1948|1948]] (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * [[:en:1949|1949]] (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * [[:en:1979|1979]] (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward antechamber)
{{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - Fukuyama City official website * [https://www.k-shinichi.com/spot?id=18 Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun |image = File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg |Other name = Osayama No. 1 Kofun | part_of = Osayama Kofun Cluster |location = {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Oaza Tote |coordinatesree = 34|coordinatesute = 33|coordinatesond = 45.00 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (or {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) |area = 12 meters per side |burial = Double-sleeved {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods Found = |Associated Mounds = |built = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} |Interred_individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun" |designation1 = |notes = |map = Japan Hiroshima |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in {{ill|1948|en|1948|WD=Q5165|lt=1948}} (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * {{ill|1948|en|1948|WD=Q5165|lt=1948}} (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|1949|en|1949|WD=Q5188|lt=1949}} (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * {{ill|1979|en|1979|WD=Q2484|lt=1979}} (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. {{small|Stone pillars on both sides.}} Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward entrance) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward main chamber) Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site: *** Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun - Designated on September 17, 1948 (Showa 23); renamed on August 2, 1949 (Showa 24)[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education website).. == References == {{Reflist}} == Sources == * Historical site intypeation board (Fukuyama City Board of Education and Shinichi Town Chamber of Commerce Youth Group, installed in 1997) * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Hiroshima Prefecture names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 35|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Hiroshima Prefecture names|1982}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=Takahiro Koto|year=1989|chapter=Shiratsuka Kofun > Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkyōdō Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun|title=Shinichi Town History|volume=Materials Volume I|publisher=Shinichi Town, Ashina District, Hiroshima Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120450.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] - |map = Japan Hiroshima |type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map_dot_label = Osayama
Shiratsuka Kofun |map_dot_label = Top |coordinates = {{Coord|34.562778|N|133.295556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architectural_styles = {{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} }} [[File:Osayama Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|View from the mound}}{{small|The Osayama Kofun Cluster is spread out below.}]]] '''Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun''' (大佐山白塚古墳, also known as Osayama No. 1 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|en|Fukuyama, Hiroshima|WD=Q661038|lt=Fukuyama}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Shinichi|ja|新市町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinichi|1=Shin'ichi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502125}} Tote area. The mound is square-shaped (or round) and is part of the Osayama Kofun Cluster. It has been Historic Site1 as a historical site by Hiroshima Prefecture. == Overview == The Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun is located near the summit of Osayama, which stands at an elevation of 188 meters, on the southern slope offering a panoramic view of the {{ill|Fukuyama Plain|lt=Fukuyama Plain|lt=Kannabe Plain|ja|神辺平野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannabe Plain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 福山平野}} to the north. It is the largest kofun in the surrounding Osayama Kofun Cluster. The site has not undergone excavation. The Kofun is square-shaped (or possibly round), measuring 12 meters per sideHistorical site intypeation board.. The base of the mound's front features a stone arrangement resembling paving and a surrounding trench at the rear{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens to the south. The chamber is finely constructed with cut {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=granite}}, and a stone pillar is placed between the main chamber (玄室) and the antechamber (羨道). Details about burial goods remain unclear. The construction period is estimated to be the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} (or mid-7th century[https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64216.html Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun] (Fukuyama City official website).). The site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site in {{ill|1948|en|1948|WD=Q5165|lt=1948}} (Showa 23). == History == * Edo period: Mentioned in {{ill|Seibi Meiku|ja|西備名区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seibi Meiku|1=Seibi Meiku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361032}}{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. * {{ill|1948|en|1948|WD=Q5165|lt=1948}} (Showa 23), September 17: Designated as a Hiroshima Prefecture Historic Site. * {{ill|1949|en|1949|WD=Q5188|lt=1949}} (Showa 24), August 2: Renaming of the Historic Site1 historical site. * {{ill|1979|en|1979|WD=Q2484|lt=1979}} (Showa 54): Survey conducted{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Top-down view of the stone chamber}]]] [[File:Osayama Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded view of the stone chamber}]]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southward. The area of the chamber are as follows: * Total length: 7.8 meters * Main chamber: Length 3.75–3.88 meters, width 1.9 meters, height 2.2 meters * Antechamber: Length approximately 4 meters The chamber features finely crafted granite blocks, with a stone pillar and lintel stone marking the transition between the antechamber and main chamber. It is considered a two-room structure, with the antechamber serving as a front room and the main chamber as a rear room{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. Similar chambers are found in the {{ill|Daibo Kofun|ja|大坊古墳 (福山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbō Kofun|qq=}} and Wolf Mound No. 2 (狼塚2号古墳) in Fukuyama. The rear wall is a single large slab, and the side walls are constructed in two tiers{{Sfn|Japanese Kofuns Encyclopedia|1989}}. The chamber's walls were likely coated with plaster, as suggested by the name "Shiratsuka" ("White Mound"), though this is not evident today. The floor appears to have been paved with gravel, supported by scattered small pebbles{{Sfn|Shinichi Town History Materials Volume I|2002}}.
